headline: How a US$4.25 Billion IP Misappropriation Lawsuit At Lockheed Martin (LMT) Has Changed Its Investment Story
summary: Recently, Napoli Shkolnik filed a US$4.25 billion federal lawsuit against Lockheed Martin and others, alleging a multi-year insider scheme to misappropriate SDR Group’s proprietary hybrid airship surveying platform and cut it out of a proposed US$3.40 billion deal. The case shines a spotlight on Lockheed Martin’s handling of intellectual property and partner relationships around its hybrid airship platform, raising questions about legal, reputational, and collaboration risks. Next, we’ll...

headline: Lockheed Martin Faces US$4.25b SDR Lawsuit And Investor Risk Questions
summary: SDR Group has filed a $4.25b federal lawsuit against Lockheed Martin, alleging misappropriation of proprietary geophysical surveying technology and business model. The complaint claims Lockheed Martin used this technology and approach in connection with its hybrid airship platform, cutting SDR Group out of a potential $3.4b deal. The case also references engagement with federal agencies, including DARPA, the Department of Commerce, and USGS, as well as international partners. For investors...
